notes: Session 8 (Opus 4.7, 1M ctx). Diagnosed + fixed image-editor slowdown that pegged memory until the OS choked. Root causes were stacked: (a) Dexie's useLiveQuery in OutputCanvas re-runs on every write, returning fresh Blob refs — the per-tile useEffect([blob]) revoked & re-created its ObjectURL on every Dexie change, forcing every to re-decode → linear cost in gallery size per write; (b) the gallery flex-wrapped ALL rows simultaneously, holding decoded textures in GPU memory for every image — unbounded; (c) jobs.byTab grew forever because done/error/cancelled jobs were never pruned and retained their AbortController refs; (d) zustand persist serialises all tabs synchronously on every prompt keystroke; (e) input_images sit in tab.params as multi-MB base64 data URIs. Shipped fixes #1+#3+#2: (1) NEW src/lib/blob-url-cache.ts — refcounted ObjectURL cache keyed by record id with 100ms delayed revoke for strict-mode double-effects; OutputCanvas GalleryTile + ImageLightbox LightboxMedia now call acquireBlobUrl(rec.id, blob)/releaseBlobUrl(rec.id) instead of churning per blob ref; effect dep flipped from [blob] → [rec.id]. (3) jobs.store.ts — added pruneTerminal() helper, addJob keeps all running + last MAX_TERMINAL_JOBS_PER_TAB=8 terminal per tab; updateJob clears merged.controller on terminal status transitions so AbortController + closures GC. (2) Gallery virtualization via IntersectionObserver: new useInView(rootMargin) hook in OutputCanvas; GalleryTile attaches tileRef to outer div, only acquires URL + renders when within 600px of viewport; when scrolled far offscreen, img unmounts → URL released → texture freed. SkeletonTile untouched (running jobs are few). Tile divs stay mounted with fixed dimensions so scroll layout never shifts. Still TODO from earlier diagnosis: #4 debounce zustand persist (every-keystroke localStorage write), #5 move input_images from base64 data-URI strings to Blob[] in a non-persisted store + render via ObjectURL (would also unblock structuredClone cost in useRunJob). Pushed at end of session.
